explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Obtain external validation of AI practices or require vendor certifications.

fairness.path_to_improvement: Document bias testing procedures or vendor AI fairness requirements.

governance.findings: Reports detail alleged unauthorized use of a public figure's likeness and voice in an advertisement, highlighting a lack of consent and control over AI-generated content. The CIO discusses cautious customer-facing generative AI deployment, referencing governance and ethics concerns.
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

relevant_sources.summary: This third-party report on "Incident 1304" provides evidence for the **governance** and **privacy** pillars. It supports governance by detailing the alleged unauthorized use of a public figure's likeness and voice in an advertisement, highlighting a lack of consent and control over AI-generated content. The incident also supports the privacy pillar by demonstrating concerns around the unauthorized alteration and use of an individual's personal data (voice and statements) without their permission.
relevant_sources.title: Incident 1304: Whirlpool Reportedly Used AI-Altered Footage of North Carolina State Senator DeAndrea Salvador in Brazilian Advertisement

relevant_sources.summary: This Fortune article provides evidence for the **governance** and **transparency** pillars of responsible AI. The CIO's discussion of cautious customer-facing generative AI deployment due to "governance and ethics concerns" directly supports the governance pillar. Furthermore, the article's mention of testing LLMs for customer service and reliance on specific vendor AI tools like Google's BigQuery with AI tools indicates a structured approach to AI implementation and use, contributing to transparency.
relevant_sources.title: Whirlpool CIO: Lessons Learned from IoT Hype Cycle Can Apply to Generative AI (Fortune)

oversight.path_to_improvement: Document human review processes for AI-assisted decisions (built or vendor AI).

privacy.findings: Reports reference concerns regarding the unauthorized alteration and use of an individual's personal data, specifically their voice and statements, without their permission.
privacy.path_to_improvement: Publish AI-specific privacy impact assessments or data handling procedures.

relevant_sources.summary: This third-party report on "Incident 1304" provides evidence for the **governance** and **privacy** pillars. It supports governance by detailing the alleged unauthorized use of a public figure's likeness and voice in an advertisement, highlighting a lack of consent and control over AI-generated content. The incident also supports the privacy pillar by demonstrating concerns around the unauthorized alteration and use of an individual's personal data (voice and statements) without their permission.
relevant_sources.title: Incident 1304: Whirlpool Reportedly Used AI-Altered Footage of North Carolina State Senator DeAndrea Salvador in Brazilian Advertisement

transparency.findings: Whirlpool describes testing large language models (LLMs) for customer service and references reliance on specific vendor AI tools, such as Google's BigQuery with AI tools. This indicates a structured approach to AI implementation and use.
transparency.path_to_improvement: Publish detailed documentation (model cards, system specs) for AI systems deployed.

relevant_sources.summary: This Fortune article provides evidence for the **governance** and **transparency** pillars of responsible AI. The CIO's discussion of cautious customer-facing generative AI deployment due to "governance and ethics concerns" directly supports the governance pillar. Furthermore, the article's mention of testing LLMs for customer service and reliance on specific vendor AI tools like Google's BigQuery with AI tools indicates a structured approach to AI implementation and use, contributing to transparency.
relevant_sources.title: Whirlpool CIO: Lessons Learned from IoT Hype Cycle Can Apply to Generative AI (Fortune)

summary.overall_findings: Drawing from 9 publicly available sources, Whirlpool's published materials address 3 of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars. Transparency, privacy, and governance are addressed at the policy level, with materials describing testing LLMs for customer service and referencing concerns around the unauthorized alteration and use of an individual's personal data without their permission. Further disclosures detail alleged unauthorized use of a public figure's likeness and voice in an advertisement. No qualifying public evidence was found for fairness, explainability, and 2 additional pillars.
